locator.quote: The £50 bonus produced FEWER closes per day (8.4) than the default

text: Probe O: the £50 close bonus produced fewer closes per day (8.4) than the default £1 (9) — the cleanest refutation of the magnitude hypothesis.

locator.quote: The meta-test conclusively rules out training length as the

text: Probe A2: doubling training 3→7 days at bonus £10 produced fewer closes (7.7), conclusively ruling out training length as the bottleneck.

locator.quote: shaped gradient against fc cost has to propagate through

text: Diagnosis: a representational bottleneck means any shaped gradient against fc cost has to propagate through hundreds of ticks against ±£500/day naked variance, which swamps it.
E2 (asymmetric MTM, drawdowns weighted 3× gains) was also NO BITE — the policy slightly reduced opens rather than substituting close_signal; same conclusion as E1.
